Clean GPS tracking data in one step

Source: R/clean_tracks.R
clean_tracks.Rd

Top-level wrapper that runs the full cleaning pipeline: column standardization/coercion, quality flagging, duplicate removal, speed filtering, optional spatial filtering, optional regularization, and interpolation.

clean_tracks(
  data,
  col_map = NULL,
  max_speed = 30,
  speed_col = "Speed",
  regularize = TRUE,
  interval_minutes = 60,
  max_gap_minutes = 180,
  land_polygon = NULL,
  flag_quality = TRUE,
  drop_flagged = FALSE,
  verbose = TRUE
)

Arguments

data

A data frame or tibble containing GPS tracking data.

col_map

Optional named character vector/list used for column standardization.

max_speed

Numeric. Maximum plausible speed.

speed_col

Character. Name of speed column.

regularize

Logical. If TRUE, run `regularize_tracks()` and `interpolate_tracks()`.

interval_minutes

Numeric. Regularization interval in minutes.

max_gap_minutes

Numeric. Maximum gap to interpolate.

land_polygon

Optional sf/sfc/Spatial object used to filter excluded spatial points.

flag_quality

Logical. If TRUE, run `flag_low_quality_fixes()`.

drop_flagged

Logical. If TRUE, remove rows flagged as low quality.

verbose

Logical. If TRUE, print progress messages.

Value

A cleaned data frame with a `cleaning_log` attribute.

Contents
